mission.title: k8up: Failed backup stated as succeeded
mission.description: Failed backup stated as succeeded. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify k8up troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your k8up deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n k8up -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8up
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8up -n k8up --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review k8up configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant k8up configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n k8up -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8up
kubectl get configmap -n k8up -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=k8up
```
Backups are stated as `Succeeded` even if the backup commands failed. This is just an example, the reason _why_ the backup failed has already been fixed. However, it is problematic to describe a defective backup as successful.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Failed backup stated as succeeded
steps.description:
```
---
apiVersion: k8up.io/v1
kind: PreBackupPod
metadata:
  name: mysqldump
spec:
  #backupCommand: sh -c 'mysqldump -u$USER -p$PW -h $DB_HOST --all-databases'
  backupCommand: 'sh -c "printf whatup && exit 1"'
  pod:
    spec:
      containers:
        - env:
            - name: MARIADB_USER
   
```yaml
❯ kubectl get backups.k8up.io -n authelia-public 
NAME                    SCHEDULE REF   COMPLETION   PREBACKUP   AGE
postgres                               Succeeded    Finished    12h
postgres-backup-qbq6q   postgres       Succeeded    Finished    12h
```

steps.title: Confirm Failed backup stated as succeeded is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8up -n k8up --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n k8up --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
```
---
apiVersion: k8up.io/v1
kind: PreBackupPod
metadata:
  name: mysqldump
spec:
  #backupCommand: sh -c 'mysqldump -u$USER -p$PW -h $DB_HOST --all-databases'
  backupCommand: 'sh -c "printf whatup && exit 1"'
  pod:
    spec:
      containers:
        - env:
            - name: MARIADB_USER
              value: root
            - name: DB_HOST
              value:
codeSnippets:
❯ kubectl get backups.k8up.io -n authelia-public 
NAME                    SCHEDULE REF   COMPLETION   PREBACKUP   AGE
postgres                               Succeeded    Finished    12h
postgres-backup-qbq6q   postgres       Succeeded    Finished    12h

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with k8up installed or the issue environment reproducible.
